title: GameStop Turnaround Strategy and Earnings Improvement Progress
text: GameStop's turnaround strategy has successfully pivoted the company toward collectibles, which now comprises over 31% of quarterly revenue. Sales in the collectibles division skyrocketed by 63% year-over-year, while hardware and accessories revenue also saw robust growth, jumping 31% to $592.1 million. CEO Ryan Cohen's strategy emphasizes cost-cutting, profitability, and a selection of higher-value items that align with GameStop's trade-in model. The company improved margins and operating income by closing underperforming stores and reducing SG&A expenses. GameStop's Q2 2025 earnings report marked a dramatic turnaround, with revenue surging 21.8% year-over-year to $972.2 million and net income jumping to $168.6 million from $14.8 million in the prior-year period. Adjusted EPS came in at $0.25, crushing estimates of $0.16 and marking a sharp improvement from $0.01 in the prior year. Operating Income reached $66.4 million for Q2 FY25, a significant turnaround from an operating loss of $22.0 million in the prior-year quarter. GameStop concluded Q1 FY25 with $6.4 billion in cash, significantly bolstered by capital raises. In March 2025, the board approved Bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset, and by June 2025, the company had acquired 4,710 Bitcoin, representing an investment exceeding half a billion dollars.
